draft_v1: {"schema_v":1,"recipe":{"title":"QUICK CHICKEN STOCK","description":"NOTE : This recipe also produces cooked chicken leg meat, which can be used in other recipes or saved for later use. Alternatively, replace the chicken legs with more backs and wingtips.","yield_phrase":"MAKES 2 QUARTS"},"steps":[{"instruction":"1. Pour 4 cups water into a medium bowl and sprinkle with the gelatin. Set aside until the gelatin is hydrated, about 10 minutes.","ingredient_lines":[{"raw_ingredient_text":"1 ounce (4 packets; about 3 tablespoons) unflavored gelatin"}]},{"instruction":"2. Meanwhile, chop the chicken backs and wing tips with a cleaver into rough 2-inch pieces, or cut up with poultry shears. Working in two or three batches, transfer the chicken to a food processor and pulse until the texture roughly approximates ground chicken, stopping if a particularly hard bone gets stuck on the blade.","ingredient_lines":[{"raw_ingredient_text":"2 pounds skin-on chicken backs and wing tips, preferably from young chickens"}]}]}
